channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Zeke Miller And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here are the billionaires stocking Trump’s next administration
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several billionaires to top roles in his next administration — a combination worth over $360 billion and that includes the world’s richest person.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden attends national Christmas tree lighting on windy Washington night
item.description: President Biden attended the national Christmas tree lighting as he prepares to depart the White House in January as the 46th President of the United States of America.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: Boston city councilor Tania Fernandes Anderson was arrested Friday on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom, the U.S. attorney's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to Europe for the 1st time following election win for Notre Dame's reopening
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will be in France this weekend to celebrate the reopening of the famed Notre Dame Cathedral alongside 50 other heads of state, marking the president-elect's first foreign trip following his election victory last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden lights National Christmas tree for last time as president
item.description: President Joe Biden presided over the lighting of the National Christmas tree for his final time as commander-in-chief on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US government is closing a women's prison and other facilities after years of abuse and decay
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal Bureau of Prisons is permanently closing its “rape club” women’s prison in California and will idle six facilities in a sweeping realignment after years of abuse, decay and mismanagement, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House rejects Democratic efforts to force release of Matt Gaetz ethics report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House shut down Democrats' efforts Thursday to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz, pushing the fate of any resolution to the yearslong investigation of sexual misconduct allegations into further uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Bitcoin tops $100K on optimism over Trump crypto plans
item.description: It last traded at $100,027 as of 0240 GMT, up 2.2% on the previous session, after earlier rising as high as $100,277.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: California lawmakers kick off special session to 'Trump-proof' the Golden State
item.description: California lawmakers on Monday are kicking off a special legislative session in what Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) has deemed efforts to "Trump-proof" the Golden State from potential federal interference.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump changes White House counsel pick, tapping David Warrington
item.description: Trump in a statement said Warrington would serve as the top attorney in the White House and as assistant to the president.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Leadership teams, committee assignments set for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Leadership positions in the Nevada Legislature are falling into place with less than three months until the 2025 session convenes in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump team signs an agreement with the DOJ for security clearances
item.description: The new agreement will help Trump officials on agency landing teams access classified information needed to prepare to take over on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions have scored a major legal victory with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: U.S. will send Ukraine $725 million more in arms
item.description: President Joe Biden has pledged to spend all of the military assistance funds Congress approved this year for Ukraine before the end of his administration on Jan. 20, 2025.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump falls just below 50% in popular vote, but gets more than in past elections
item.description: This year's popular-vote margin is the second-closest since 1968 and still tightening. It shows just how closely divided the country is politically, and that any shift to the right is marginal.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: At Supreme Court, vaping may be frowned upon, but that could change with Trump
item.description: The focal point of the case is 2009 law enacted by Congress that gives the Food and Drug Administration a mandate to curb the availability of nicotine products for minors.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Some Democrats are frustrated over Joe Biden reversing course and pardoning his son Hunter
item.description: Already reeling from their November defeat at the polls, Democrats now are grappling with President Joe Biden's pardoning of his son for a federal felony conviction — after the party spent years slamming Donald Trump as a threat to democracy who operates above the law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC
